clauses.obligation: 20-year term from FA execution date. Must open within 12 months or FA expires (extension available for $1,000).
8% of gross sales payable weekly via pre-authorized bank draft. Gross sales = all sales of every kind from restaurant.
4.5% of gross sales payable weekly to SFAFT.
Franchisee has sole responsibility for finding location. Franchisor approves/rejects. Must submit location approval request.
Must build out per Operations Manual standards (Fresh Forward 2.0). All costs at franchisee sole expense.
Must complete training program (40hr classroom + 60hr OTJ). Failure to complete = non-curable default.
Must operate in strict compliance with Operations Manual. Manual is confidential trade secret. Franchisor may modify at any time.
Must purchase all products exclusively from approved sources. May not independently source.
Must submit weekly sales reports via POS system. Must maintain accurate records. Franchisor may audit.
Must carry GL ($2M per occurrence/$4M aggregate), WC, Auto ($1M), and property insurance. Must use Gold Standard Insurance Program. Must name franchisor and affiliates as additional insureds.
Must defend and indemnify franchisor, affiliates, SUIPH, SFAFT, BDs, agents, representatives, directors, officers, employees against any claims arising in connection with restaurant operation.
10 days to cure payment defaults; 30 days to cure other defaults.

Immediate termination for 14 categories: failure to open, misrepresentation, felony, trade secret breach, abandonment (2 biz days), unauthorized transfer, underreporting >3%, insolvency, trademark misuse, repeated failures, health/safety, immediate safety, failure to pay third parties, dishonest conduct.
De-identification within 14 days; return Operations Manual; assign telephone/domain/social media; quit and surrender premises; pay all sums including lost future royalties; turn over all materials.
Franchisor has right (not duty) to purchase equipment, supplies, inventory at fair market value within 30 days of termination. May set off amounts owed. Goodwill of marks excluded from FMV.

No transfer before opening. ROFR 30 days. 9 conditions: compliance with ROFR, assumption of obligations, all amounts paid, buyer trained, buyer signs then-current FA, transfer fee paid, premises possession, general release signed, comply with survival terms.
No direct or indirect association with competitive business anywhere during term.
1 year, 3-mile radius from any Subway that operates or operated in prior year.
$15,000 per competing business plus 8% of its gross sales. Plus injunction and/or damages.
Must maintain all confidential/proprietary information during and after term. No disclosure except to employees with need to know.
Principal owners must personally guarantee all obligations. Guaranty covers FA, Sublease payment, all other obligations.
Mandatory arbitration via AAA or ADRC in Connecticut. FAA governs. No court litigation except injunction for IP/confidentiality/noncompete. Claims limited to DAL only — not affiliates or individuals.
No special, incidental, consequential, punitive, or multiple damages except where prohibited by law. Defaulting party under arbitration clause pays other party's expenses.
Florida law governs. FAA governs arbitration.
Each individual signing as franchisee is jointly and severally liable.

All improvements, developments, inventions, copyrights, works of authorship by franchisee or employees relating to Subway are owned by franchisor. Irrevocable power of attorney to execute assignment documents.
